channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Hundreds of troops kicked out under 'don't ask, don't tell' get upgraded to honorable discharges
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon announced Tuesday that more than 800 military personnel have seen their service records upgraded to honorable discharges after previously being kicked out of the military under its former “don’t ask, don’t tell” policy.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Harris steps up efforts to reach Black men
item.description: Vice President Harris is stepping up her outreach to Black men amid concerns that her support with the voting bloc is softening. Harris is sitting down with Charlamagne tha God in Detroit on Tuesday and announced a raft of measures Monday aimed at appealing to the group.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Laredo enters its sixth day of a boil-water notice after E. coli is discovered in water system
item.description: The South Texas city is the latest to grapple with aging water infrastructure, which officials suggested could have been the cause for the E. coli outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Hurricane misinformation stokes fears ahead of election
item.description: The flood of conspiracy theories and rumors following the severe weather in the South is sparking concerns that political figures’ embrace of misinformation is just the beginning as the general election draws closer.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump uses interview on economics to promote tariffs and riff on his favorite themes
item.description: Donald Trump has seized on an opening to sound his frequent argument that imposing huge tariffs on foreign goods would amount to an economic elixir — one he claims would raise enormous sums for the government, protect U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: From cease-fire push to boots on the ground in Israel: US seemingly accepts involvement in escalating war
item.description: The U.S. is now more deeply entrenched in the conflict in the Middle East after a pivotal move to send troops to Israel was announced this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Harris zeroes in on Black men as she and Republican Donald Trump seek to fire up key voting blocs
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Vice President Kamala Harris stopped by a Detroit art gallery Tuesday accompanied by three Hollywood stars for a conversation with Black men about entrepreneurship as both she and Donald Trump sought to energize key constituencies their allies worry may be slipping away.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pandas arrive at National Zoo, but won't be ready for public debut until January
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The National Zoo's long dark panda drought has come to an end. Eleven months after the zoo sent its three wildly popular pandas — Mei Xiang, Tian Tian and their cub Xiao Qi Ji — back to China, a new pair of bears arrived late Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New report warns bloodthirsty Venezuelan gang's footprint will remain in US ‘for decades'
item.description: Tren de Aragua, a violent Venezuelan gang that has sparked national headlines, will likely remain in the U.S. for decades, according to a new report being released this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says it would be a 'smart thing' if he spoke to Putin, though he won't confirm he has
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Former President Donald Trump on Tuesday refused to say whether he's spoken with Russian President Vladimir Putin since leaving office, as reported in journalist Bob Woodward's latest book. But if the two did speak, Trump said, it would be “a smart thing” for the United States.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tillis warns conservatives’ 'unwise' reforms would 'weaken' Senate GOP
item.description: North Carolina Sen. Thom Tillis (R) circulated a “Dear Colleague” letter Tuesday warning that some of the reforms being pushed by Senate conservatives would only weaken the next Senate Republican leader and cause the same chaos that is plaguing the House Republican majority.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Migrant deaths in New Mexico have increased tenfold
item.description: Experts say smugglers are treating migrants more harshly and bringing them on paths that could be more dangerous in extreme summer temperatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: US law entitles immigrant children to an education. Some conservatives say that should change
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — At a sparsely attended meeting last year, the Saugus Public School Committee approved a new admissions policy, it said, to streamline the process of enrolling students.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and JOCELYN GECKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rally arrest: Vem Miller says it's 'false allegations'
item.description: A self-proclaimed "kindergarten kid when it comes to gun," Miller claims the charges against him are all "false.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Harris brings a bit of Trump into her rally to try to show him as ‘unhinged’
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris wants voters to pay more attention when Trump talks about “the enemy within.” On Monday, she played a highlight reel to paint him as “increasingly unstable and unhinged.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram and Danielle Kurtzleben, MPR News

item.title: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez, challenger Joe Kent face off in Washington 3rd District debate
item.description: One of the races that could determine the fate of Congress this election season happens to be the rematch race for Washington's 3rd District, and on Monday night, the candidates will debate on KOIN 6.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: Election Day Looms: Hurricane impacts on voting
item.description: Voting rules changed for impacted Florida counties after hurricane Helene, now the question is, will rules be adjusted after hurricane Milton?
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: One-on-one with Mississippi's candidates in 2024 US Senate race
item.description: JACKSON, Miss. (WJTV) - U.S. Senator Roger Wicker (R-Miss.) and his Democratic opponent, Ty Pinkins, spoke to WJTV 12 News ahead of the November General Election. Wicker, a Pontotoc native, has been serving Mississippi in the U.S. Senate since 2007.
item.dc:creator: Tom Wright, NewsNation

item.title: Native Voters could swing Arizona. Both parties want their votes.
item.description: Both Republicans and Democrats are trying to marshal Native American voters in Arizona, which could prove decisive to winning the key state.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Will Donald Trump and Kamala Harris debate again?
item.description: While there was originally talk of more than one meeting between Trump and Harris, the former president made his intentions clear with a post on Truth Social.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, NewsNation

item.title: Harris gives Democrats a boost, but control of House up for grabs
item.description: Election experts are pointing to data saying Harris has clearly been an overall asset to the Democrats’ downballot prospects.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Clyburn calls Project 2025 a 'Jim Crow 2.0'
item.description: Rep. James Clyburn (D-S.C.) on Sunday called the conservative policy agenda Project 2025 a “Jim Crow 2.0.” Clyburn said on CNN's "State of the Union" that he was the ninth Black lawmaker elected to Congress from South Carolina — almost 100 years after the previous Black lawmaker was elected.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Are political disagreements stressing you out? Here are tips to bridge the divide
item.description: With only weeks to a divisive election it can be hard to talk politics. Polarization can damage our relationships and our health. We have strategies to reduce election stress, starting with ourselves.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey, MPR News

item.title: What we know about arrest outside Trump’s California rally
item.description: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, a noted supporter of Trump, discussed safety measures taken at the Saturday event.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Democratic voter registration raises red flags for Harris
item.description: Voter registration shifts in key battleground states are raising concerns for Democrats and suggesting Vice President Harris faces challenges in Pennsylvania, North Carolina, Arizona and Nevada.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Riverside Co. sheriff says deputies 'probably' prevented third assassination attempt on Trump
item.description: A man was arrested outside of a campaign rally for former President Donald Trump in Southern California, and multiple guns were illegally in his possession, according to authorities. The arrest took place at a checkpoint at the intersection of Avenue 52 and Celebration Drive in Coachella at 4:59 p.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, NewsNation

item.title: Catholic League accuses Whitmer of mocking Eucharist with Dorito
item.description: Podcaster Liz Plank posted a video on her Instagram showing herself being fed a Dorito by Whitmer, who's standing above Plank.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Who is not allowed to vote in the 2024 presidential election?
item.description: The United States is just weeks away from a highly-anticipated presidential election, but not everyone will be able to exercise their right to vote on Nov. 5.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, PIX 11

item.title: Walz returns to Mankato to reprise role as coach, deliver football pep talk and campaign a little
item.description: The governor stopped through on a campaign visit marking the 25th anniversary of the season the Mankato West football team won the state championship with him as assistant coach. He will attend the Governors Pheasant opener in Sleepy Eye Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Prepared to sit out election over war abroad, a young Minnesota voter finds her way back into the fold
item.description: As the Democratic presidential ticket changed, a young voter came around to supporting it despite her frustrations with U.S. policy toward war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Ellie Roth, MPR News

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: NJ assemblyman's car stolen, house broken into
item.description: HAZLET, N.J. (PIX11) - Video from the home where a New Jersey assemblyman is currently living shows alleged burglars break into the home and speed away with his personal car - complete with State Assembly plates.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Mayor Frey vetoes carbon emission fee adopted by city council
item.description: In his veto letter Minneapolis Mayor Jacob Frey said he agrees with the aims of the fee on polluters, but he said the council’s action amounts to an “unauthorized tax.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, Fox 5

item.title: Biden postpones trip to Germany and Angola to monitor Hurricane Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden said Tuesday he is postponing a planned trip to Germany and Angola to stay at the White House to monitor Hurricane Milton, as it spins closer to Florida’s Gulf Coast.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Election drop boxes open in San Diego County
item.description: While mail ballots are expected to arrive this week for registered voters, 150 official ballot drop boxes opened Tuesday in San Diego County for the November Presidential General Election.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, Fox 5
